# Figure 3. Spatial organization of Hofbauer cells in the placental villous stroma revealed by Stereo-seq.

**(A)** Spatial transcriptomic maps of 16 mid-gestation (GW18+6 to GW24+1) placental basal plate sections generated by Stereo-seq (bin50, ~50 μm diameter). For each sample, left panel: all ten annotated cell types mapped by color; right panel: Hofbauer cells (HB, red) highlighted against all other cells (grey). Sample ID and gestational week are indicated. HB cells concentrate in villous stromal regions and are largely absent from the extravillous trophoblast (EVT) shell and deep decidua.

**(B)** High-magnification (40×) zoom-in of a representative villous cross-section (~180 μm diameter) from Sample 011 (GW20+5). HB cells (red) are tightly apposed to fetal capillaries, with direct cell-to-vessel distances below 5 μm in most villi. HB cells are surrounded by fibroblasts (FB, green) and fetal vascular endothelial cells (fVEC, blue), forming a "FB-encases-HB, HB-abuts-vessel" spatial sandwich. The SCT/VCT boundary is delineated by blue dashed lines enclosing the villous stroma.

**(C)** Neighborhood enrichment analysis of cell types within a 50-μm radius of each Hofbauer cell. Bar plot shows the observed/expected ratio (enrichment) for each cell type compared to 10,000 random spatial permutations. FB shows the strongest enrichment (2.77-fold, p < 10⁻⁴, Bonferroni-corrected), followed by fVEC/fEC (1.91-fold, p < 10⁻⁴). SCT (0.31-fold) and VCT (0.44-fold) are significantly depleted, consistent with an 80–120 μm spatial separation between the trophoblast layer and the stromal core. Maternal immune cells (dNK, CD14_M) are near-absent in HB neighborhoods (enrichment < 0.1). Red bars, enriched (enrichment > 1.2); blue bars, depleted (enrichment < 0.8); grey bars, not significant.

**Supplementary Figure 3A.** Cell type composition heatmap across all 16 Stereo-seq samples, with the HB column highlighted in red. Values represent percentage of each cell type per sample. The HB proportion ranges from ~3% to ~8% across samples, confirming consistent representation.

**Supplementary Figure 3B.** Dot plot of canonical Hofbauer cell markers (FOLR2, CD163, CD68, C1QA, C1QB, C1QC, SPP1, FN1, etc.) in the Stereo-seq data, confirming the molecular identity of spatially annotated HB cells.

**Supplementary Figure 3C.** Villous zoom plots for Samples 010 (GW20+3) and 011 (GW20+5), showing consistent HB spatial organization across independent samples. FB, fVEC, and HB co-localize within the villous stroma, while SCT/VCT form the outer boundary.

**Supplementary Figure 3D.** Spatial maps of all 16 samples on dark background, highlighting HB cells in bright red. These images emphasize the non-random, stromal-confined distribution of HB cells across all gestational ages examined.
